Package org.solenopsis.soap.metadata

Class FlowWaitEvent

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.FlowBaseElement
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.FlowElement
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.FlowWaitEvent
public class FlowWaitEvent extends FlowElement

Java class for FlowWaitEvent complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="FlowWaitEvent">
   <complexContent>
     <extension base="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}FlowElement">
       <sequence>
         <element name="conditionLogic" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="conditions" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}FlowCondition" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="connector" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}FlowConnector" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="eventType" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="inputParameters" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}FlowWaitEventInputParameter" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="label" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="maxBatchSize" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}int" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="offset" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}int" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="offsetUnit" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}FlowScheduledPathOffsetUnit" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="outputParameters" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}FlowWaitEventOutputParameter" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="resumeDate" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}date" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="resumeDateReference" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="resumeTime" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}time" minOccurs="0"/>
       </sequence>
     </extension>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

  • Method Details

    • getConditionLogic

      public String getConditionLogic()
      Gets the value of the conditionLogic property.
      Returns:
      possible object is String

    • setConditionLogic

      public void setConditionLogic(String value)
      Sets the value of the conditionLogic property.
      Parameters:
      value - allowed object is String

    • getConditions

      public List<FlowCondition> getConditions()
      Gets the value of the conditions property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the conditions property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getConditions().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list FlowCondition

      Returns:
      The value of the conditions property.

    • getConnector

      public FlowConnector getConnector()
      Gets the value of the connector property.
      Returns:
      possible object is FlowConnector

      Sets the value of the connector property.
      Parameters:

    • getInputParameters

      public List<FlowWaitEventInputParameter> getInputParameters()
      Gets the value of the inputParameters property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the inputParameters property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getInputParameters().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list FlowWaitEventInputParameter

      Returns:
      The value of the inputParameters property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the outputParameters property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows:

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list FlowWaitEventOutputParameter

      Returns:
